summary: Iteration 34 — Frontend-only testing of rewritten registration form (Username + Confirm Password + Age checkbox). All form fields render correctly and in correct order. Validation logic correctly blocks API calls for all invalid inputs. Successful registration redirects to /(tabs)/home. API error extraction is correct (returns string, not [object Object]). CRITICAL BUG: Alert.alert() in react-native-web 0.21.0 is a complete no-op — users receive ZERO visual feedback for ALL validation errors and API errors on web.

ui_bugs.issue: Alert.alert() in react-native-web 0.21.0 is a no-op (empty function body: `static alert() {}`). ALL validation error messages are silently swallowed. Users get zero feedback when: empty fields submitted, passwords mismatch, age not checked, username invalid, duplicate email/username returned from API. Form just silently does nothing.
ui_bugs.selector: N/A — affects all Alert.alert() calls in register.tsx lines 33, 39, 44, 49, 66

issues: Design looks clean and correct: dark background, white labels, muted placeholders, good contrast. Username * required indicator in red. Helper text muted. Optional label muted. No design issues found.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: react-native-web v0.21.0 (the version installed in this project) removed the Alert implementation. The file /app/frontend/node_modules/react-native-web/dist/exports/Alert/index.js contains: `class Alert { static alert() {} }` — a completely empty function that does nothing.
reproduction_steps: 4. Observe: nothing happens, no error message shown (should show 'Please fill in all required fields')
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Replace all Alert.alert() calls in register.tsx with a web-compatible solution. Options: (1) Use Platform.OS check: `if (Platform.OS === 'web') { window.alert(title + '\n' + message); } else { Alert.alert(title, message); }` OR (2) Use inline error state with useState and render error text below the form OR (3) Use a toast library like react-native-toast-message that works on web

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix Alert.alert() no-op on web — replace all Alert.alert() calls in frontend/app/auth/register.tsx with a web-compatible alternative. Affected lines: 33, 39, 44, 49, 66. Recommended: use Platform.OS check or inline error state messages.
Consider adding inline error messages below each field (better UX) instead of alert dialogs for: empty fields, password mismatch, age not checked, invalid username, duplicate email/username
critical_code_review_comments: register.tsx uses Alert.alert() for ALL error feedback (7 calls). react-native-web 0.21.0 has Alert.alert = no-op. This is a critical UX regression — zero feedback on web.

The error extraction pattern `error?.response?.data?.detail || error?.message || 'Something went wrong'` is correct and returns a proper string (not [object Object]). API errors are properly extracted but never displayed due to Alert no-op.
Username validation regex `/^[a-z0-9_]{3,20}$/` is correct and works as intended (logic verified).

success_rate.frontend: 45% — form renders correctly, validation logic is correct, happy path works, but ALL error messages are invisible to users due to Alert.alert no-op
